rulings.Case Title: IVAN VALLADARES CRUZ ET AL VS. VICTOR SERRATO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 22, 2017 line 4. DEFENDANT VICTOR SERRATO's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT GRANTED. Punitive damages are not available for breach of implied contractual covenant. Spinks v. Equity Residential (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th. To the extent Plaintiff is asserting a trespass cause of action (Opposition 3:15-28), Plaintiff has a leave to amend to so allege.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MIKHAIL TARNOPOLSKIY VS. NEBOJSA T. VELJIC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 22, 2017 line 12. DEFENDANT NEBOJSA VELJIC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend. Notice is fatally defective. Per SFRO 37.9(c) requires that the recovery of possession be based on one of the permissible grounds and that notice be given of the grounds under which possession is sought. The ground for eviction per SFRO 37.9(a)(8) is for the "landlord" to recover possession in good faith, without ulterior reasons and with honest intent. The operative Notice as drafted asserts that the grounds for eviction are "relative's" good faith and honest intent, which is not one of the permissible grounds for eviction under SFRO, i.e. the tenant is not informed in writing of the grounds for termination of tenancy as required.= (501/REQ)
